title: Why Are Only 3% of Preschool Teachers Men?
subtitle: 幼教性别失衡之谜

paragraphs.en: Nationwide, only about three percent of early childhood teachers are male in the U.S. Experts say this can have an impact on young children whose understanding of gender roles and identity are rapidly forming. Research has found that having access to diverse teachers is beneficial for children.
paragraphs.zh: 全美范围内，仅有约3%的幼儿教师为男性。专家指出，这对性别角色与身份认知正快速成型的幼儿可能产生影响。研究表明，接触多元背景的教师对儿童成长颇为有益。

paragraphs.en: For the youngest learners, it means they are more likely to get exposed to different varieties of play and communication. It also helps them develop healthy ideas around gender.
paragraphs.zh: 对最年幼的学习者而言，这意味着他们更有可能接触到不同类型的游戏与交流方式，同时也有助于他们形成关于性别的健康观念。

paragraphs.en: "In our world and our society, we have very specific stereotypes of gender roles," said Mindi Reich-Shapiro, an assistant professor in the teacher education department of the Borough of Manhattan Community College, and one of the authors of a recent study. "It's important for children to see other possibilities and other paths they can take."
paragraphs.zh: 曼哈顿社区学院教师教育系助理教授、近期一项研究的合著者明迪·赖希-夏皮罗表示：“在我们的世界与社会中，关于性别角色存在着非常刻板的印象。让孩子们看到其他可能性与可选择的其他道路，这一点至关重要。”

words.meaning: n. 刻板印象，模式化观念

paragraphs.en: Despite mostly feeling supported by colleagues and family members, many of the male educators surveyed in the study reported facing social or cultural resistance in their careers as early education teachers. Some also reported that there were parents surprised or concerned that their child had a male teacher. And they had been advised by colleagues or other staff not to hug children.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管多数受访男性幼教工作者感觉获得了同事与家人的支持，但许多人表示在职业生涯中仍遭遇社会或文化上的阻力。一些人还提到，曾有家长对自己孩子有位男老师感到惊讶或忧虑。他们甚至还被同事或其他工作人员提醒不要拥抱儿童。

paragraphs.en: Reich-Shapiro and fellow researchers made several recommendations to increase male representation in the field. Low pay has long been acknowledged as a major issue in the early childhood field. Over 70% of male educators who said they intended to stay in the early education workforce noted an increased salary was a major motivating factor for them to commit to the career long-term.
paragraphs.zh: 赖希-夏皮罗及其同事提出了若干建议以提高该领域男性从业比例。低薪问题向来被视为幼教领域的主要困境。在表示愿意留任幼教队伍的男性教师中，超过七成指出加薪是促使他们长期投入该职业的主要动力。

paragraphs.en: The report suggests paying all early childhood educators the way elementary school teachers are paid. Cities and programs should establish support groups for male early childhood educators and provide mentoring and professional development advice for male educators and their program leaders.
paragraphs.zh: 报告建议，所有幼儿教育工作者应享有与小学教师同等的薪酬待遇。各城市与相关项目应为男性幼教老师建立支持小组，并为其本人及项目主管提供指导与职业发展方面的建议。

paragraphs.en: The authors also suggest that traditional recruitment approaches for early childhood educators do not address the gender gap in the field. They recommend providing young men opportunities to work with children through training and volunteer programs, targeting groups of men who are considering a career change, such as fathers.
paragraphs.zh: 作者还指出，传统的幼教师资招聘方式未能解决该领域的性别失衡问题。他们建议通过培训与志愿项目为年轻男性提供与儿童共事的机会，并将目标锁定在正考虑转行的男性群体，例如父亲们。
